code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values - remove quotes from strings
    def clean_value(val: str) -> str:
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (the one with most triples)
    main_subject = max(subject_triples.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_triples[s]))

    # Predicate to text mappings with better sentence structures
    predicate_templates = {
        "author": lambda s, o: f"written by {o}",
        "isbnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with ISBN {o}",
        "followedBy": lambda s, o: f"followed by {o}",
        "precededBy": lambda s, o: f"preceded by {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"in the {o}",
        "mediaType": lambda s, o: f"{o} format",
        "numberOfPages": lambda s, o: f"{o} pages",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"from {o}",
        "publisher": lambda s, o: f"published by {o}",
        "genre": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genre",
        "literaryGenre": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genre",
        "releaseDate": lambda s, o: f"released on {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"born on {o}",
        "spokenIn": lambda s, o: f"spoken in {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{o} ethnicity",
        "abbreviation": lambda s, o: f"abbreviated as {o}",
        "firstPublicationYear": lambda s, o: f"first published in {o}",
        "issnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with ISSN {o}",
        "codenCode": lambda s, o: f"with CODEN {o}",
        "LCCN_number": lambda s, o: f"with LCCN {o}",
        "eissnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with eISSN {o}",
        "frequency": lambda s, o: f"published {o}",
        "academicDiscipline": lambda s, o: f"covering {o}",
        "editor": lambda s, o: f"edited by {o}",
        "impactFactor": lambda s, o: f"with impact factor {o}",
        "oclcNumber": lambda s, o: f"with OCLC {o}",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": lambda s, o: f"classified as {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"headquartered in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"led by {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {o}",
        "affiliation": lambda s, o: f"affiliated with {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"located in {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"in {o}",
        "influencedBy": lambda s, o: f"influenced by {o}",
        "family": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} family",
        "genus": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genus",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"serves {o}",
        "founder": lambda s, o: f"founded by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"capital is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"known as {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"leader title is {o}",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"nicknamed {o}",
        "notableWork": lambda s, o: f"known for {o}",
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"attended {o}",
        "doctoralAdvisor": lambda s, o: f"advised by {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"from {o}",
        "residence": lambda s, o: f"resides in {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"subsidiary of {o}",
    }

    # Build the main sentence for the primary subject
    main_triples = subject_triples[main_subject]

    if not main_triples:
        return main_subject

    # Start with the subject name
    parts = [main_subject]

    # Get predicates for main subject
    for t in main_triples:
        if t.predicate in predicate_templates:
            clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
            template = predicate_templates[t.predicate]
            parts.append(template(main_subject, clean_obj))
        else:
            parts.append(f"{t.predicate} {clean_value(t.object)}")

    # Join all parts into a fluent sentence
    sentence = " ".join(parts)

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

The original code was:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values - remove quotes from strings
    def clean_value(val: str) -> str:
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Group triples by subject to build coherent sentences
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Templates for predicates - using relative clauses for natural flow
    templates = {
        "author": "{subject} was written by {object}",
        "isbnNumber": "with ISBN {object}",
        "followedBy": "followed by {object}",
        "precededBy": "preceded by {object}",
        "language": "written in {object}",
        "mediaType": "{object} format",
        "numberOfPages": "{object} pages",
        "country": "published in {object}",
        "publisher": "published by {object}",
        "genre": "a {object} work",
        "literaryGenre": "in the {object} genre",
        "releaseDate": "released on {object}",
        "birthDate": "born on {object}",
        "spokenIn": "{object} is spoken in {subject}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{object} ethnic group",
        "abbreviation": "abbreviated as {object}",
        "firstPublicationYear": "first published in {object}",
        "issnNumber": "with ISSN {object}",
        "codenCode": "with CODEN {object}",
        "LCCN_number": "with LCCN {object}",
        "eissnNumber": "with eISSN {object}",
        "frequency": "published {object}",
        "academicDiscipline": "covering {object}",
        "editor": "edited by {object}",
        "impactFactor": "with impact factor {object}",
        "oclcNumber": "with OCLC {object}",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": "classified as {object}",
        "headquarter": "headquartered in {object}",
        "leader": "led by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "born in {object}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {object}",
        "affiliation": "affiliated with {object}",
        "city": "located in {object}",
        "state": "in {object} state",
        "influencedBy": "influenced by {object}",
        "family": "family {object}",
        "genus": "genus {object}",
        "regionServed": "serves {object}",
        "founder": "founded by {object}",
        "capital": "capital is {object}",
        "demonym": "known as {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {object}",
        "nickname": "nicknamed {object}",
        "notableWork": "known for {object}",
        "almaMater": "attended {object}",
        "doctoralAdvisor": "advisor was {object}",
        "nationality": "from {object}",
        "residence": "resides in {object}",
        "parentCompany": "subsidiary of {object}",
    }

    # Build main subject clause
    main_subject = triples[0].subject if triples else ""
    main_clauses = []
    other_clauses = []

    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
            clause = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=clean_obj)
        else:
            clause = f"{t.predicate} {clean_value(t.object)}"

        # Identify main work subject vs related subjects
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            other_clauses.append(clause)

    # Build the main sentence starting with subject and first predicate
    if not main_clauses:
        return main_subject

    # First clause includes subject, subsequent clauses use relative pronouns
    first_clause = main_clauses[0]
    remaining_clauses = main_clauses[1:]

    # Connect remaining clauses with relative pronouns for natural flow
    if remaining_clauses:
        # Determine appropriate relative pronoun based on content
        if any("written" in c or "published" in c or "released" in c for c in remaining_clauses):
            connector = " which is "
        else:
            connector = ", "

        # Build the sentence with proper connectors
        sentence = first_clause
        for i, clause in enumerate(remaining_clauses):
            if i == 0:
                sentence += connector + clause
            else:
                sentence += ", " + clause
    else:
        sentence = first_clause

    # Add other subject clauses as additional information
    if other_clauses:
        sentence += ", " + ", ".join(other_clauses)

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values - remove quotes from strings
    def clean_value(val: str) -> str:
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (the one with most triples)
    main_subject = max(subject_triples.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_triples[s]))

    # Predicate to text mappings with better sentence structures
    predicate_templates = {
        "author": lambda s, o: f"written by {o}",
        "isbnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with ISBN {o}",
        "followedBy": lambda s, o: f"followed by {o}",
        "precededBy": lambda s, o: f"preceded by {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"in the {o}",
        "mediaType": lambda s, o: f"{o} format",
        "numberOfPages": lambda s, o: f"{o} pages",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"from {o}",
        "publisher": lambda s, o: f"published by {o}",
        "genre": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genre",
        "literaryGenre": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genre",
        "releaseDate": lambda s, o: f"released on {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"born on {o}",
        "spokenIn": lambda s, o: f"spoken in {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{o} ethnicity",
        "abbreviation": lambda s, o: f"abbreviated as {o}",
        "firstPublicationYear": lambda s, o: f"first published in {o}",
        "issnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with ISSN {o}",
        "codenCode": lambda s, o: f"with CODEN {o}",
        "LCCN_number": lambda s, o: f"with LCCN {o}",
        "eissnNumber": lambda s, o: f"with eISSN {o}",
        "frequency": lambda s, o: f"published {o}",
        "academicDiscipline": lambda s, o: f"covering {o}",
        "editor": lambda s, o: f"edited by {o}",
        "impactFactor": lambda s, o: f"with impact factor {o}",
        "oclcNumber": lambda s, o: f"with OCLC {o}",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": lambda s, o: f"classified as {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"headquartered in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"led by {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {o}",
        "affiliation": lambda s, o: f"affiliated with {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"located in {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"in {o}",
        "influencedBy": lambda s, o: f"influenced by {o}",
        "family": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} family",
        "genus": lambda s, o: f"of the {o} genus",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"serves {o}",
        "founder": lambda s, o: f"founded by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"capital is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"known as {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"leader title is {o}",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"nicknamed {o}",
        "notableWork": lambda s, o: f"known for {o}",
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"attended {o}",
        "doctoralAdvisor": lambda s, o: f"advised by {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"from {o}",
        "residence": lambda s, o: f"resides in {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"subsidiary of {o}",
    }

    # Build the main sentence for the primary subject
    main_triples = subject_triples[main_subject]
    
    if not main_triples:
        return main_subject

    # Start with the subject name
    parts = [main_subject]
    
    # Get predicates for main subject
    for t in main_triples:
        if t.predicate in predicate_templates:
            clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
            template = predicate_templates[t.predicate]
            parts.append(template(main_subject, clean_obj))
        else:
            parts.append(f"{t.predicate} {clean_value(t.object)}")
    
    # Join all parts into a fluent sentence
    sentence = " ".join(parts)
    
    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."
